expressions.english: small sound
small city
small problem
small mistake
to be short (in height)
the clothes are small
the voice is small
You can fit into an outfit this small?
My inferiority complex is that I am short.
expressions.notes: 콤플렉스 = is something one feels self-conscious about. It usually includes a body part and/or appearance.

expressions.english: My friends tease me for being short.
expressions.notes:
친구 = friend
-들 = is a suffix used to indicate plural.
놀리다 = to tease; to make fun of; to mock

expressions.english: I have a small wish.
expressions.notes:
소망 = hope, wish
하나 = one (native Korean number)
있다 = to be there; to exist; to have

expressions.korean: 이 치마는 저한테 너무 작은 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I think this skirt is too small for me.
expressions.notes:
치마 = skirt
-것 같다 = it seems/looks like + S + V

expressions.korean: 저는 키가 작아서 항상 하이힐만 신어요.
expressions.english: I am short, so I always wear high heels.
expressions.notes:
항상 = always, all the time
하이힐 = high heels
-만 = only; just
신다 = to wear (footwear/socks)

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 키는 작은데 목소리는 정말 커요.
expressions.english: He's short (in height), but has a really big voice.
expressions.notes:
그 사람 = that person; he, she
정말 = really; very
크다 = to be big; to be tall; to be loud

expressions.korean: 차가 너무 작아서 사람이 많이 못 탈 것 같아요.
expressions.english: The car is too small, so I don't think many people can get in it.
expressions.notes: 타다 = to ride; to get on; to take (vehicle)

expressions.korean: 가방이 너무 작아서 물건을 많이 넣을 수가 없어요.
expressions.english: The bag is too small, so I can't put many things in it.
expressions.notes:
물건 = thing, stuff; item; belonging
넣다 = to put (something) in
-(으)ㄹ 수 없다 = to be not able to + V, can't

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 목소리가 너무 작아서 말을 알아듣기 힘들어요.
expressions.english:
His voice is too small, so it's hard to understand his words.
He speaks too softly, so it's hard to understand what he's saying.
expressions.notes:
말 = language; what one says; expression; word, term
알아듣다 = to understand what one says

expressions.korean: 이 컴퓨터는 화면이 너무 작아서 보고 있으면 눈이 아파요.
expressions.english: This computer has such a small screen that my eyes hurt when I look at it.
expressions.notes:
컴퓨터 = computer
화면 = screen
-고 있다 = to be + V-ing
눈 = eye
아프다 = to be sick, hurt

expressions.korean: 그렇게 작은 체구에 어디서 그런 힘이 나오는지 모르겠어요.
expressions.english: I don't know where such strength comes from within such a small body.
expressions.notes:
그렇게 = like that
체구 = build, frame, the size of one's body
어디서 = where
어디서 = is originally 어디에서, but people often just use 어디서 for the ease of pronunciation.
그런 = (something) like that; such + noun
힘 = strength, power, energy
나오다 = to come out

expressions.korean: 이 신발은 너무 작아서 저한테 안 맞아요. 조금 더 큰 걸 보여주세요.
expressions.english: These shoes don't fit me because they are too small. Please show me something a bit bigger.
expressions.notes:
조금 = a little
큰 = is the adjective form of 크다.
보여주다 = to show, display
